Unable to access the database encryption key because the OS encryption keyring backend has changed from {previousBackend} to {currentBackend}. This can occur if the desktop environment changes, for example between GNOME and KDE.

Please switch to the previous desktop environment or try to run signal with the command line flag --password-store="{previousBackendFlag}"
signal.systemEncryptionError__linuxSafeS:
Unable to decrypt the database encryption key using the OS encryption keyring. This can occur in certain containerized environments such as Flatpak.

We recommend quitting and checking your OS encryption backend such as gnome-libsecret or kwallet, then trying again.

You may choose to continue anyway, however the database key will be stored in plaintext on the filesystem and other apps may be able to access it.

signal.SafetyTipsModal__Description: Be careful when accepting message requests from people you don’t know. Watch out for:
signal.SafetyTipsModal__TipDescription--: Signal will never contact you for your registration code or PIN. Be cautious of requests that impersonate others. Profile names are chosen by their account holder and aren't verified.
If someone you don’t know messages about cryptocurrency (like Bitcoin) or a financial opportunity, be careful—it’s likely spam.
Spammers often start with a simple message like “Hi” to draw you in. If you respond they may engage you further.
Be careful of messages from people you don’t know that have links to websites. Never visit links from people you don’t trust.
Be careful of businesses or government agencies contacting you. Messages involving tax agencies, couriers, and more can be spam.

signal.PreferencesDonations__description: Donate to support private messaging. Keep Signal independent and ad-free. <readMoreLink>Read more</readMoreLink>

signal.PreferencesDonations__privacy-mod: <paragraph>Signal does not collect or store any of your personal information when you make a donation.</paragraph><paragraph>We use Stripe as our payment processor to receive your donations. We don't access, store, or save any of the information you provide to them.</paragraph><paragraph>Signal does not and cannot connect your donation to your Signal account.</paragraph><paragraph>Thank you for your support!</paragraph>
As an independent nonprofit, Signal is committed to private messaging and calls. No ads, no trackers, no surveillance. Donate today to support Signal.
Get an optional badge on your profile when you donate
Signal is a 501c3 nonprofit. US donations are tax deductible.
